summary: Use parameterized query to fix SQL injection in get_user

operations.new_body:
cursor.execute("SELECT * FROM users WHERE username = ?", (username,))
return cursor.fetchone()

test_spec.setup_code:
import sqlite3
conn = sqlite3.connect(":memory:")
cursor = conn.cursor()
cursor.execute("CREATE TABLE users (username TEXT)")
cursor.execute("INSERT INTO users VALUES (?)", ("alice",))

rationale: User input was interpolated into the SQL string. Replaced with a parameterized query (qmark style) so the value is bound, not parsed as SQL. A test exercising the classic OR-1=1 payload now returns None (no match) instead of returning a row, proving the injection is closed.
